heading: Military and civilian partnership for trauma readiness grant program
label: 300d–91 Military and civilian partnership for trauma readiness grant program

children.heading: Military trauma team placement program
children.label: a Military trauma team placement program

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Assistant Secretary for Preparedness and Response and in consultation with the Secretary of Defense, shall award grants to not more than 20 eligible high-acuity trauma centers to enable military trauma teams to provide, on a full-time basis, trauma care and related acute care at such trauma centers.

children.label: A
children.text: shall be for a period of at least 3 years and not more than 5 years (and may be renewed at the end of such period); and

children.label: B
children.text: shall be in an amount that does not exceed $1,000,000 per year.

children.heading: Availability of funds
children.label: 3 Availability of funds
children.text: Notwithstanding or any other provision of law, funds available to the Secretary for obligation for a grant under this subsection shall remain available for expenditure for 100 days after the last day of the performance period of such grant.section 1552 of title 31

children.heading: Military trauma care provider placement program
children.label: b Military trauma care provider placement program

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Assistant Secretary for Preparedness and Response and in consultation with the Secretary of Defense, shall award grants to eligible trauma centers to enable military trauma care providers to provide trauma care and related acute care at such trauma centers.

children.label: A
children.text: shall be for a period of at least 1 year and not more than 3 years (and may be renewed at the end of such period); and

children.label: i
children.text: $100,000 for each military trauma care provider that is a physician at such eligible trauma center; and

children.label: ii
children.text: $50,000 for each other military trauma care provider at such eligible trauma center.

children.label: A
children.text: be deployed by the Secretary of Defense for military operations, for training, or for response to a mass casualty incident; and

children.label: B
children.text: be deployed by the Secretary of Defense, in consultation with the Secretary of Health and Human Services, for response to a public health emergency pursuant to .section 247d of this title

children.heading: Use of funds
children.label: 2 Use of funds
children.text: Grants awarded under this section to an eligible trauma center may be used to train and incorporate military trauma care providers into such trauma center, including incorporation into operational exercises and training drills related to public health emergencies, expenditures for malpractice insurance, office space, information technology, specialty education and supervision, trauma programs, research, and applicable license fees for such military trauma care providers.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: d Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to affect any other provision of law that preempts State licensing requirements for health care professionals, including with respect to military trauma care providers.

children.heading: Report to the Secretary and the Secretary of Defense
children.label: 1 Report to the Secretary and the Secretary of Defense
A
children.text: the number and types of trauma cases managed by military trauma teams or military trauma care providers pursuant to such grant during such year;

children.label: B
children.text: the ability to maintain the integration of the military trauma providers or teams of providers as part of the trauma center, including the financial effect of such grant on the trauma center;

children.label: C
children.text: the educational effect on resident trainees in centers where military trauma teams are assigned;

children.label: D
children.text: any research conducted during such year supported by such grant; and

children.label: E
children.text: any other information required by the Secretaries for the purpose of evaluating the effect of such grant.

children.label: A
children.text: maintaining military trauma care providers’ readiness and ability to respond to and treat battlefield injuries;

children.label: B
children.text: providing health care to civilian trauma patients in urban and rural settings;

children.label: C
children.text: the capability of trauma centers and military trauma care providers to increase medical surge capacity, including as a result of a large-scale event;

children.label: D
children.text: the ability of grant recipients to maintain the integration of the military trauma providers or teams of providers as part of the trauma center;

children.label: E
children.text: efforts to incorporate military trauma care providers into operational exercises and training and drills for public health emergencies; and

children.label: F
children.text: the capability of military trauma care providers to participate as part of a medical response during or in advance of a public health emergency, as determined by the Secretary, or a mass casualty incident.

children.label: A
children.text: Such trauma center has an agreement with the Secretary of Defense to enable military trauma teams to provide trauma care and related acute care at such trauma center.

children.label: B
children.text: At least 20 percent of patients treated at such trauma center in the most recent 3-month period for which data are available are treated for a major trauma at such trauma center.

children.label: C
children.text: Such trauma center utilizes a risk-adjusted benchmarking system and metrics to measure performance, quality, and patient outcomes.

children.label: i
children.text: affiliated with a medical school;

children.label: ii
children.text: that maintains residency programs and fellowships in critical trauma specialties and subspecialties, and provides education and supervision of military trauma team members according to those specialties and subspecialties; and

children.label: iii
children.text: that undertakes research in the prevention and treatment of traumatic injury.

children.label: E
children.text: Such trauma center serves as a medical and public health preparedness and response leader for its community, such as by participating in a partnership for State and regional hospital preparedness established under section 247d–3b or 247d–3c of this title.

children.label: A
children.text: Such trauma center has an agreement with the Secretary of Defense to enable military trauma care providers to provide trauma care and related acute care at such trauma center.

children.label: B
children.text: Such trauma center utilizes a risk-adjusted benchmarking system and metrics to measure performance, quality, and patient outcomes.

children.label: C
children.text: Such trauma center demonstrates a need for integrated military trauma care providers to maintain or improve the trauma clinical capability of such trauma center.

children.heading: Major trauma
children.label: 3 Major trauma
children.text: The term “major trauma” means an injury that is greater than or equal to 15 on the injury severity score.

children.heading: Military trauma team
children.label: 4 Military trauma team
children.text: The term “military trauma team” means a complete military trauma team consisting of military trauma care providers.

children.heading: Military trauma care provider
children.label: 5 Military trauma care provider
children.text: The term “military trauma care provider” means a member of the Armed Forces who furnishes emergency, critical care, and other trauma acute care services (including a physician, surgeon, physician assistant, nurse, nurse practitioner, respiratory therapist, flight paramedic, combat medic, or enlisted medical technician) or other military trauma care provider as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: g Authorization of appropriations
children.text: To carry out this section, there is authorized to be appropriated $11,500,000 for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023.
